one_line_value_proposition: Physiotherapist-guided digital osteoarthritis programme with NICE HTG766 and NG226 recommendations — structured exercise and education for hip and knee OA at scale.
why_it_matters_locally: MSK conditions are the largest single cause of disability and a major driver of GP and orthopaedic demand. Joint Academy provides a physiotherapist-guided digital programme for hip and knee OA patients, reducing pressure on face-to-face physiotherapy services and orthopaedic waiting lists.
target_problem_statement: Long orthopaedic waiting lists, limited physiotherapy capacity, and inadequate structured support for osteoarthritis self-management between appointments.
evidence_summary: NICE HTG766 recommendation. Multiple peer-reviewed studies supporting physiotherapist-guided digital OA management including RCTs demonstrating pain reduction and functional improvement.
live_sites: Multiple NHS organisations and international deployments across Europe and North America
patients_covered_note: Over 100,000 patients treated globally. Growing NHS deployment base.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with Joint Academy before commissioning.
cyber_notes: Swedish-headquartered company — confirm data residency and DPA terms for NHS use.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting location and DPA terms with Joint Academy. Swedish-headquartered company.
target_patients: Adults with mild to moderate hip or knee osteoarthritis suitable for conservative management.
exclusions: Not indicated for patients requiring urgent surgical intervention or those with contraindications to exercise.
onboarding_detail: Clinician or physiotherapist referral. Patient accesses app-based programme with assigned physiotherapist guidance.
supplier_wrap: Joint Academy provides physiotherapist support, digital programme and outcome reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Low local effort. Referral pathway required. Analytics dashboard available for population monitoring.
training_note: Minimal training required for referring clinicians.
monitoring_model: Guided self-help with physiotherapist oversight. Not continuously monitored.
monitoring_note: Guided self-help with physiotherapist check-ins. Not a continuously monitored clinical service.
escalation_note: Escalation to clinical services as clinically indicated. Programme includes safety screening.
indicative_price_text: £200–£350 per patient. Contact Joint Academy for NHS volume pricing.
service_wrap_note: Physiotherapist support and analytics included in per-patient fee.
contract_note: Contact Joint Academy for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Evidence of clinically meaningful pain reduction and functional improvement in OA patients. Potential to reduce orthopaedic referrals and physiotherapy demand.
tariff_considerations: Model using orthopaedic outpatient and physiotherapy appointment costs. Joint replacement deferral is a relevant long-term outcome.
roi_note: Reduction in physiotherapy and orthopaedic appointments is the primary ROI driver. Joint replacement deferral provides additional long-term savings.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Referral pathway with MSK first-contact practitioners or GPs. Minimum 200 patient cohort for meaningful outcome data.
procurement_notes: Direct contract with Joint Academy. Confirm G-Cloud availability.
demo_notes: Contact Joint Academy for a demonstration.
source_summary: NICE HTG766, NICE NG226, Joint Academy website.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information. Verify current assurance, pricing and data residency with Joint Academy before procurement.
